# LinkedList

> Growable data structures comprising small objects connected together by means of pointers

## Definition
- Store data in separate nodes(boxes), each node hold a specific pointer to the next node
- Box: a container holding a value and a slot for an arrow
- Variable names: lables that hold the starting arrow for a list
## Singly Linked Lists
- disadvantage: fully implementing the Java List interface. eg: *previous* operation
```java
class IntList {
	int first;
	IntList rest; //IntList is the class, and its a reference. That one node whose value is first and whose tail is another IntList. So if you say Node rest, then you need to define class Node first
	IntList(int first, Node rest) {
		this.first = first;
		this.rest = rest; //pointer
	}
	
}

IntList L1 = IntList.list(1, 2, 3);
IntList L2 = new IntList(4, L1.rest);
```

- Reference: 存引用，new的结果一定是reference
```java
Entry a = new Entry(); //a is a reference that points to one Entry subject
a.next = b;
String s = "hi";
int[] arr = new int[3]; //任何对象类型？

```
- Primitive: 存值
```java
int x = 5;
double d = 3.2;
boolean ook = true;
char c = 'A';
```
## Sentinels
- A node 
## Doubly Linked Lists
```java
class Node {
	int item;
	Node prev;
	Node next;
}

new.prev = p;
new.next = p.next;
```
- 更占内存
